words: This interview with Mi Lew and Marie Lee Lew took place at their home on the
edge of Moscow, Idaho on December 10, 1975. Interviewer is SAM SCHRAGER.
MI LEW: —give credit to anybody then he goes broke, he himself goes broke,
very seldom get anything out of anybody. I read in the paper where
W. T. Grant in the middlewest or someplace thejf creditor was supposed
to collect, the store close up and nobody— I think he's gonna have a
hard time. My father-in-law had a restaurant in Ellensburg. I think
all those people there, those hay you know, every spring they
come hay, you know, cut hay and they work maybe one month, six weeks,
you know, and then they pay. But in the meantime he closed up, he
closed up and I sent a lawyer to ask for collection. I think out of
four hundred dollars, I think he got eighteen dollars, altogether.
And no use, they had to run all the way from here over to Ellensburg.
and I could see it ain't no use.

words: Who did you go to to try to get a job?

words: First, in order to get a job you have to stay there for a little while.

speaker: ML
words: Then pretty soon you have to know some friends. The Chinese are very

words: good. Supposing my wife's friend now, that knows your relative, then

speaker: ML
words: the relative goes to the other one, and say a good word for you, and

speaker: ML
words: then they'll go ahead and then, okay, we'll try and see. And maybe

speaker: ML
words: he will come back,— a certain commission will go back to the— off

speaker: ML
words: of your wages, go back to the other guy, too. You don't know that.

speaker: ML
words: If you don't know that, see, you're fine, because you don't feel guilty.

speaker: ML
words: But if you feel that you're supposed to make five hundred dollars,

speaker: ML
words: you got a hundred dollars go back to the other one, then you feel bad,

speaker: ML
words: because if he told you only four hundred dollars, he'd be okay,

speaker: ML
words: see. That's psychological, lit does work that way. The Chinese people h

words: do do that, see.

speaker: ML
words: you don't know the head man you have to get to the lower ones, and grad

words: ually go in that way. It's not easy. If you're relative to one, you

speaker: ML
words: don t need to know anything, you.get to the top, you can get in there

speaker: ML
words: to the head man. I guess that's the same way all over.

speaker: MLL

words: while you get five hundred dollar maybe you give the engineer three

speaker: MLL
words: hundred dollar^ and you keep the two hundred dollar. You have a few

speaker: MLL

speaker: MLL
words: self.

speaker: SS
words: Without working too much!

speaker: ML

words: poor, that are trying to do something for themselves, but yet they can't

speaker: ML
words: help themselves. I do have a little softness,that way, but I do hate.

words: work then give 'em something to eat, see. There are a lot of 'em

words: get a meal." But there a lot of people said, "Can you give me a meal,

words: work, you know, if there's any work or not to be done after he eats,

words: that's another thing.

speaker: ML
words: I imagine fifteen or sixteen year old kid— he want^ to work. I say,

speaker: ML
words: "Would you want a little work?" He said, "Yes," he wanted to work.

speaker: ML
words: My goodness, I give him some potatoes, old-time we haven't got potatoes

words: like we do now so I give him a little bucket and,peel potatoes.

speaker: ML
words: I feel that wouldn't take too much effort, if he was hungry he was wil

speaker: ML
words: ling to peel the potatoes, because it wasn't that much effort to do.

speaker: ML
words: If I go and tell him chop wood or dig ditch then he^ be so hungry he

speaker: ML
words: couldn't do it. But this kid was really a very nice kid. After he

speaker: ML
words: peeled the potatoes he wanted to do something else, so I fed him, after

speaker: ML

speaker: ML
words: other work to be done. And he did, he did all the work I waited himML/MLL 16

speaker: ML
words: to do. Then, he didn't ask for pay, but I paid him for the work, for

speaker: ML
words: the work, see, I knew it was more than his food was, and I paid him

speaker: ML

speaker: ML
words: there's a lot of 'em that you feed 'em— just as soon as you feed 'em

speaker: ML
words: they'll peel mayDe a quarter of a bucket, and then pretty soon, you

speaker: ML
words: don't look, they go out through the back door and you don't see 'em at

speaker: ML
words: all. You don't see 'em at all. They leave the potato, and leave it

speaker: ML
words: there and beat it. You know, the world's funny that way.

speaker: ML
words: in the old-times, one hour for the meal. And we consider always that.

speaker: MLL
words: of us girls born already, see, and when the first boy came that's the

speaker: MLL

words: anymore. I want to move." So, we all packed up and getting ready to

words: move to New York, maybe going into something else. Anyway, he want to
